MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Deborah Fox for special administrator Debra Dolch (via Zoom).
Sheila Robello for petitioner Nahia West (via Zoom).
John DiAna for Stephanie Windham, personal representative of the Estate of Jean A. Windham (via Zoom).

The Court congratulated counsel on reaching settlement. A discussion was then held regarding an issue raised by the Probate Examiner regarding a signature on the settlement agreement. Arcadia Maximo signed the agreement on November 10, 2025 as "administrator of the estate of Cecie West"; however, her attorney Howard E. Kain did not update his client's title on his signature line. After discussing options, all counsel agreed they had no objection to the Court proceeding on the agreement as submitted. The Court took the Petition to Approve Settlement under submission with the intent to grant.

As a result of the approval of the settlement agreement, the Court took the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed filed by Nahia West, and the Petition for Letters of Administration filed by Phyllis Whitefield and Myra Maximo, off calendar.

The Amended Petition for Letters of Aministration with Will Annexed filed by Debra Dolch was taken under submission with the intent to grant. The Court noted that objections previously filed by Ms. Whitfield had been withdrawn in light of the settlement.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

------------------------------------------------------------------
POST-SCRIPT, 11/19/2025:

The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The will dated May 9, 2022 is admitted to probate. Debra J. Dolch is appointed as the Administrator with will annexed with full authority. Bond is set at $600,000. Order for the same signed in open Court. A status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for April 19, 2027, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. If either petition is filed two weeks prior, no appearance is required on that date. Michael Kasolas is appointed probate referee. DISCLOSURE: The assigned probate referee is married to a legal advisory staff member of this court.

At the court's query, Ms. Robello offers an update regarding settlement discussions. Mr. Burrey offers an update regarding the will contest and the issue of service re the same. At the court's query, counsel confirms that this matter should be ready for trial setting.

After additional discussion, including confirmation of a date that this matter will be set for trial, the individual Petitions for Letters of Administration are set for trial in the department of the Presiding Judge (206) on Aug-11-2025 at 9:30 a.m., order for the same signed in open court. The Status of Trial Setting is taken off calendar. The order setting trial is briefly summarized. Counsel is advised that a copy of the same shall be available on the court's website, shortly.

Mr. Madison's absence is noted. Attorney Jonathan Madison is ordered to appear on Aug-11-2025 at 9:00 am in Department 204 to explain why he has not paid sanctions as ordered at the hearing on Apr-01-2025. An Order to Show Cause as to why the court should not order Jonathan Madison to pay additional sanctions of $250 for failure to comply with previous sanction order is set for the same date.

At the court's query, Mr. Madison offers a report re his failure to appear at the previous calling. After considering the preceding, the court orders Mr. Madison to pay sanctions in the amount of $250 for failure to appear. The Order to Show Cause RE sanctions is taken off calendar. The court reminds Mr. Burrey to file the Substitution of Attorney to formalize his representation of Ms. Whitfiled in these matters.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the status of the pending matters and whether the related petitions are ready to be set for trial once again.
